rawText:
We have what may be an extremely difficult problem with an unknown time to solve it, on which quite possibly the entire future of humanity depends. — Nick Bostrom

Welcome to Part 2 of the "Wait how is this possibly what I'm reading I don't get why everyone isn't talking about this" series. Part 1 started innocently enough, as we discussed Artificial Narrow Intelligence, or ANI, and how it's all around us in the world today. We then examined why it was such a huge challenge to get from ANI to Artificial General Intelligence, or AGI, and we discussed why the exponential rate of technological advancement we've seen in the past suggests that AGI might not be as far away as it seems. Part 1 ended with me assaulting you with the fact that once our machines reach human-level intelligence, they might immediately do this:

This left us staring at the screen, confronting the intense concept of potentially-in-our-lifetime Artificial Superintelligence, or ASI, and trying to figure out which emotion we were supposed to have on as we thought about that.

Before we dive into things, let's remind ourselves what it would mean for a machine to be superintelligent. A key distinction is the difference between speed superintelligence and quality superintelligence. Often, someone's first thought when they imagine a super-smart computer is one that's as intelligent as a human but can think much, much faster — they might picture a machine that thinks like a human, except a million times quicker, which means it could figure out in five minutes what would take a human a decade. That sounds impressive, and ASI would think much faster than any human could — but the true separator would be its advantage in intelligence quality, which is something completely different.

What makes humans so much more intellectually capable than chimps isn't a difference in thinking speed — it's that human brains contain a number of sophisticated cognitive modules that enable things like complex linguistic representations or long-term planning or abstract reasoning, that chimps' brains do not. Speeding up a chimp's brain by thousands of times wouldn't bring him to our level — even with a decade's time, he wouldn't be able to figure out how to use a set of custom tools to assemble an intricate model, something a human could knock out in a few hours.

There are worlds of human cognitive function a chimp will simply never be capable of, no matter how much time he spends trying. But it's not just that a chimp can't do what we do, it's that his brain is unable to grasp that those worlds even exist — a chimp can become familiar with what a human is and what a skyscraper is, but he'll never be able to understand that the skyscraper was built by humans. In his world, anything that huge is part of nature, period, and not only is it beyond him to build a skyscraper, it's beyond him to realize that anyone can build a skyscraper. That's the result of a small difference in intelligence quality.

overview.title: The AI Revolution: Our Immortality or Extinction (Part 2)

sentences.original: We have what may be an extremely difficult problem with an unknown time to solve it, on which quite possibly the entire future of humanity depends.
sentences.translation: 우리에게는 해결 시간이 불확실한 극도로 어려운 문제가 있으며, 이 문제에 인류의 미래 전체가 달려 있을 수도 있습니다.

grammarElements.text: what may be an extremely difficult problem
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (what 관계대명사절)

grammarElements.text: with an unknown time to solve it
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (수식)

grammarElements.text: on which quite possibly the entire future of humanity depends
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (전치사 + which)

vocabulary.example: The invention of writing changed the course of humanity.
The exam was extremely difficult.
Success depends on hard work.

expressions.usage: 불확실성을 포함하여 신중하게 주장을 제시할 때
격식 있는 관계절에서 전치사를 관계대명사 앞에 배치하는 학술적 문체

patterns.pattern: We have [X], on which [Y] depends.
patterns.explanation: "전치사 + which" 관계절은 격식체에서 사용하며, 문장에 무게를 더한다. 일상체에서는 "which Y depends on"으로 전치사를 뒤에 놓는다.
examples: We face a challenge on which the survival of many species depends.
This is a decision on which our entire strategy depends.

drillQuestions.question: We have a problem _____ which the future depends.
It may be an _____ difficult problem.

sentences.notes: Nick Bostrom의 인용문으로 글 전체의 톤을 설정한다. "on which ... depends"는 전치사를 관계대명사 앞에 놓는 격식 구조(fronted preposition)로, 학술 영어의 특징이다. 일상 영어에서는 "which the entire future of humanity depends on"이 더 자연스럽다.

koreanAlignment.en: We have
what may be an extremely difficult problem
with an unknown time to solve it
on which quite possibly
the entire future of humanity depends
koreanAlignment.ko: 인류의 미래 전체가 달려 있을 수도 있는

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 에피그래프(epigraph) — 권위 있는 인용으로 글의 주제와 무게감을 설정

sentences.original: Part 1 started innocently enough, as we discussed Artificial Narrow Intelligence, or ANI, and how it's all around us in the world today.
sentences.translation: 파트 1은 충분히 무해하게 시작했습니다. 인공 특화 지능, 즉 ANI와 그것이 오늘날 우리 주변 어디에나 있다는 것을 논의하면서 말이죠.

grammarElements.text: as we discussed Artificial Narrow Intelligence, or ANI
grammarElements.label: 종속절 (as — 동시 동작/이유)

grammarElements.text: and how it's all around us in the world today
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (discussed의 목적어 — 간접의문문)

vocabulary.example: The conversation started innocently but turned serious.
Artificial intelligence is transforming many industries.
The AI has a narrow focus on one task.

expressions.usage: 나중에 심각해지는 상황의 시작을 가볍게 묘사할 때. 복선을 깔 때 자주 사용.
어떤 것이 편재함을 강조할 때

patterns.pattern: [Subject] started innocently enough, as we [past tense verb] ...
patterns.explanation: "innocently enough"는 이후에 전개될 심각한 내용에 대한 복선. 가벼운 시작과 무거운 결론의 대비를 만든다.
examples: The meeting started innocently enough, as we discussed quarterly sales.
The experiment started innocently enough, as they tested basic hypotheses.

drillQuestions.question: Part 1 started _____ enough.
ANI is all _____ us in the world today.

sentences.notes: "innocently enough"는 뒤에 더 심각한 내용이 올 것임을 암시하는 복선(foreshadowing)이다. "or ANI"는 삽입구로 약어를 소개하는 형태. Tim Urban의 유머러스한 글쓰기 스타일이 잘 드러난다.

koreanAlignment.en: Part 1 started innocently enough
as we discussed
Artificial Narrow Intelligence, or ANI
how it's all around us in the world today
koreanAlignment.ko: 그것이 오늘날 세계에서 우리 주변 어디에나 있다는 것을

sentences.original: We then examined why it was such a huge challenge to get from ANI to Artificial General Intelligence, or AGI, and we discussed why the exponential rate of technological advancement we've seen in the past suggests that AGI might not be as far away as it seems.
sentences.translation: 그런 다음 ANI에서 인공 일반 지능, 즉 AGI로 가는 것이 왜 그렇게 거대한 도전인지 살펴보았고, 과거에 우리가 목격한 기하급수적인 기술 발전 속도가 AGI가 보이는 것만큼 멀지 않을 수 있음을 시사하는 이유를 논의했습니다.

grammarElements.text: why it was such a huge challenge
grammarElements.label: 간접의문문 (examined의 목적어)

grammarElements.text: to get from ANI to AGI
grammarElements.label: to부정사 (진주어)

grammarElements.text: the exponential rate of technological advancement we've seen in the past
grammarElements.label: 주어 (관계대명사 목적격 생략)

grammarElements.text: suggests that AGI might not be as far away as it seems
grammarElements.label: 동사 + that절

vocabulary.example: The company experienced exponential growth.
Technological advancement has accelerated.
We need to examine the evidence carefully.

expressions.usage: "such + a/an + 형용사 + 명사" 구조로 강조

expressions.phrase: not as far away as it seems
expressions.usage: "as ... as" 비교 구문의 부정으로 기대와 현실의 차이를 표현

patterns.pattern: The [adjective] rate of [noun] suggests that [X] might not be as [adjective] as it seems.
patterns.explanation: 데이터/추세를 근거로 직관과 다른 결론을 제시하는 학술적 논증 패턴.
examples: The rapid rate of climate change suggests that the crisis might not be as distant as it seems.
The accelerating rate of innovation suggests that disruption might not be as unlikely as it seems.

drillQuestions.question: The _____ rate of advancement is remarkable.
AGI might not be as far away _____ it seems.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 매우 길고 복잡한 구조. "the exponential rate of technological advancement we've seen in the past"에서 관계대명사(that/which)가 생략되어 있다. "not as far away as it seems"는 겉보기와 실제의 차이를 표현하는 관용적 구문.

koreanAlignment.en: We then examined
why it was such a huge challenge
to get from ANI to AGI
the exponential rate of technological advancement
we've seen in the past
suggests that AGI might not be as far away as it seems
koreanAlignment.ko: AGI가 보이는 것만큼 멀지 않을 수 있음을 시사합니다

sentences.original: Part 1 ended with me assaulting you with the fact that once our machines reach human-level intelligence, they might immediately do this:
sentences.translation: 파트 1은 우리의 기계가 인간 수준의 지능에 도달하면 즉시 이것을 할 수 있다는 사실로 여러분을 공격하며 끝났습니다:

grammarElements.text: once our machines reach human-level intelligence
grammarElements.label: 종속절 (once — 시간)

vocabulary.example: The news assaulted us with shocking details.
Achieving human-level AI is a major milestone.
The system responded immediately.

expressions.usage: 자조적 유머로 이전 글의 충격적 결론을 되돌아볼 때. "목적격 + 동명사" 구조에 주목.
"once"는 "when"보다 확실한 미래 사건을 전제할 때 사용

patterns.pattern: [Subject] ended with [someone] [verb]-ing [someone] with the fact that ...
patterns.explanation: 이전 논의의 결론을 회고적으로 요약하면서, 동명사 구문으로 행위를 서술하는 패턴.
examples: The lecture ended with the professor shocking us with the fact that most stars are dead.
The documentary ended with the narrator confronting us with the fact that time is running out.

drillQuestions.question: Part 1 ended with me _____ you with the fact.
_____ our machines reach human-level intelligence, things will change.

sentences.notes: "me assaulting you"는 동명사의 의미상 주어가 목적격(me)인 비격식 구조. 격식체에서는 "my assaulting you"로 쓴다. Tim Urban의 자조적 유머가 잘 드러나는 문장으로, "assault"라는 강한 단어를 의도적으로 선택해 독자의 충격을 유머러스하게 표현했다.

koreanAlignment.en: Part 1 ended with
me assaulting you
with the fact that
once our machines reach human-level intelligence
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리의 기계가 인간 수준의 지능에 도달하면

koreanAlignment.en: they might immediately do this

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 자조적 유머(self-deprecating humor) — 자신의 글쓰기를 "공격"으로 묘사하며 독자와의 유대감 형성

sentences.original: This left us staring at the screen, confronting the intense concept of potentially-in-our-lifetime Artificial Superintelligence, or ASI, and trying to figure out which emotion we were supposed to have on as we thought about that.
sentences.translation: 이것은 우리를 화면을 응시하며, 우리 생애에 실현될 수 있는 인공 초지능(ASI)이라는 강렬한 개념에 직면하게 했고, 그것에 대해 생각할 때 어떤 감정을 느껴야 하는지 파악하려 하게 만들었습니다.

grammarElements.text: confronting the intense concept of ... ASI
grammarElements.label: 목적격 보어 (현재분사, 병렬)

grammarElements.text: and trying to figure out which emotion we were supposed to have on
grammarElements.label: 목적격 보어 (현재분사, 병렬)

vocabulary.example: We must confront the reality of climate change.
The debate was intense and heated.
Superintelligence could surpass all human cognitive abilities.

expressions.usage: "leave + 목적어 + -ing"는 어떤 상태에 놓아둔다는 의미. 감정적 영향을 표현할 때 효과적.
불확실한 상황에서 답을 찾으려 할 때
감정을 옷처럼 입는 것에 비유하는 창의적 표현

patterns.pattern: This left us [verb]-ing ... , [verb]-ing ... , and trying to [verb] ...
patterns.explanation: "leave + 목적어 + 현재분사" 구조의 병렬. 세 가지 상태를 나열하여 독자의 복합적 반응을 묘사.
examples: The news left us wondering about the future, questioning our assumptions, and trying to process the information.

drillQuestions.question: This left us _____ at the screen.
We were trying to figure _____ which emotion to feel.

sentences.notes: "potentially-in-our-lifetime"은 하이픈으로 연결된 복합형용사로 ASI를 수식한다. "have on"은 감정을 옷에 비유하는 독특한 표현으로, Tim Urban의 창의적 문체를 보여준다. "which emotion we were supposed to have on"에서 "supposed to"는 당위성을 나타낸다.

koreanAlignment.en: This left us staring at the screen
confronting the intense concept
of potentially-in-our-lifetime ASI
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리 생애에 실현될 수 있는 ASI의

koreanAlignment.en: and trying to figure out
which emotion we were supposed to have on

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 감정적 몰입(emotional immersion) — 독자를 "우리"로 포함하여 집단적 경험으로 만듦

sentences.original: Before we dive into things, let's remind ourselves what it would mean for a machine to be superintelligent.
sentences.translation: 본격적으로 들어가기 전에, 기계가 초지능적이라는 것이 무엇을 의미하는지 스스로 상기해 봅시다.

grammarElements.text: what it would mean for a machine to be superintelligent
grammarElements.label: 간접의문문 (remind의 목적어)

vocabulary.example: Let's dive into the details.
Please remind me to call her.
A superintelligent machine could solve any problem.

expressions.usage: 깊이 있는 논의를 시작하기 전 전환 표현
독자와 함께 배경 지식을 환기할 때

patterns.pattern: Before we [verb], let's [verb] ourselves what ...
patterns.explanation: 본론에 들어가기 전에 배경지식을 환기하는 전형적 전환 패턴. 글과 강연 모두에서 자주 사용.
examples: Before we discuss the solution, let's remind ourselves what the problem is.
Before we move on, let's ask ourselves what we've learned.

drillQuestions.question: _____ we dive into things, let's review.
Let's remind _____ what it means.

sentences.notes: "dive into"는 물에 뛰어드는 이미지에서 파생된 비유적 표현. "what it would mean for a machine to be"에서 "for a machine"은 to부정사의 의미상 주어. would는 가정법으로, 아직 현실이 아닌 가능성을 표현.

koreanAlignment.en: Before we dive into things
let's remind ourselves
what it would mean
for a machine to be superintelligent

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 전환(transition) — 새로운 섹션으로의 자연스러운 전환, 독자의 주의를 환기

sentences.original: A key distinction is the difference between speed superintelligence and quality superintelligence.
sentences.translation: 핵심적인 구분은 속도 초지능과 질적 초지능의 차이입니다.

grammarElements.text: the difference between speed superintelligence and quality superintelligence
grammarElements.label: 보어 (between A and B)

vocabulary.example: There is a clear distinction between the two concepts.
The quality of the work was outstanding.

expressions.usage: 논증에서 중요한 개념적 차이를 소개할 때

expressions.phrase: the difference between A and B
expressions.usage: 두 개념을 대조하여 설명할 때 사용하는 기본 패턴

patterns.pattern: A key distinction is the difference between [X] and [Y].
patterns.explanation: 논증의 핵심 개념을 정의하는 학술적 패턴. 두 가지를 대비하여 명확한 구분을 세운다.
examples: A key distinction is the difference between correlation and causation.
A key distinction is the difference between knowing and understanding.

drillQuestions.question: A key _____ is the difference between the two types.
The difference _____ speed and quality is important.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 글의 핵심 논제를 설정하는 주제문(topic sentence). "speed superintelligence"와 "quality superintelligence"는 Tim Urban이 Bostrom의 개념을 쉽게 풀어쓴 용어이다.

koreanAlignment.en: A key distinction
is the difference between
speed superintelligence
and quality superintelligence

sentences.original: Often, someone's first thought when they imagine a super-smart computer is one that's as intelligent as a human but can think much, much faster.
sentences.translation: 종종, 사람들이 초스마트 컴퓨터를 상상할 때 처음 떠올리는 것은 인간만큼 지능적이지만 훨씬, 훨씬 더 빠르게 생각할 수 있는 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: when they imagine a super-smart computer
grammarElements.label: 종속절 (시간 — when)

grammarElements.text: one that's as intelligent as a human
grammarElements.label: 보어 (one = thought, 관계대명사절)

grammarElements.text: but can think much, much faster
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (but)

vocabulary.example: Can you imagine life without the internet?
People envision super-smart robots in the future.
Dolphins are highly intelligent animals.

expressions.usage: 일반적인 사람들의 직관적 반응을 대변할 때
"much"의 반복으로 정도를 극적으로 강조

patterns.pattern: [Someone's] first thought when they [verb] is [one/something] that ...
patterns.explanation: 사람들의 일반적 직관을 소개한 뒤, 이후에 그것을 반박하는 논증 패턴의 전반부.
examples: Most people's first thought when they hear AI is a robot that looks human.
A student's first thought when they face a test is one that involves memorization.

drillQuestions.question: Someone's first thought is one that's _____ intelligent as a human.
It can think much, much _____.

sentences.notes: "one"은 "a computer/thought"를 대신하는 부정대명사. "as intelligent as a human"은 원급 비교. "much, much faster"에서 much의 반복은 Tim Urban 특유의 구어체 강조법이다.

koreanAlignment.en: Often, someone's first thought
when they imagine a super-smart computer
is one that's as intelligent as a human
but can think much, much faster
koreanAlignment.ko: 훨씬, 훨씬 더 빠르게 생각할 수 있는 것입니다

sentences.original: They might picture a machine that thinks like a human, except a million times quicker, which means it could figure out in five minutes what would take a human a decade.
sentences.translation: 그들은 인간처럼 생각하되 백만 배 더 빠른 기계를 상상할 수 있으며, 이는 인간이 10년 걸릴 일을 5분 만에 알아낼 수 있다는 것을 의미합니다.

grammarElements.text: a machine that thinks like a human
grammarElements.label: 목적어 (관계대명사절 포함)

grammarElements.text: which means it could figure out in five minutes
grammarElements.label: 비제한적 관계대명사절 (which)

grammarElements.text: what would take a human a decade
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (what — figure out의 목적어)

vocabulary.example: Picture yourself on a beach.
I need to figure out a solution.
Technology has changed dramatically in the last decade.

expressions.usage: 과장법으로 극단적인 속도 차이를 강조

expressions.phrase: what would take a human a decade
expressions.usage: "what + would take"로 가정적 소요 시간을 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] could [verb] in [short time] what would take [someone] [long time].
patterns.explanation: 시간 대비를 통해 능력의 차이를 극적으로 보여주는 패턴.
examples: AI could process in seconds what would take a researcher months.
The software could analyze in minutes what would take a team weeks.

drillQuestions.question: It could figure _____ in five minutes what takes a decade.
They might _____ a machine that thinks like a human.

sentences.notes: "except a million times quicker"는 문법적으로 비표준적인 삽입구로, 구어적 문체의 특징. "what would take a human a decade"에서 what은 관계대명사로 "the thing that"을 의미. take + 사람 + 시간 구조.

koreanAlignment.en: They might picture
a machine that thinks like a human
except a million times quicker
koreanAlignment.ko: 백만 배 더 빠른 것을 제외하면

koreanAlignment.en: which means
it could figure out in five minutes
what would take a human a decade

sentences.original: That sounds impressive, and ASI would think much faster than any human could — but the true separator would be its advantage in intelligence quality, which is something completely different.
sentences.translation: 그것은 인상적으로 들리며, ASI는 어떤 인간보다도 훨씬 빠르게 생각할 것입니다 — 하지만 진정한 차별화 요소는 지능의 질에서의 우위이며, 이것은 완전히 다른 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: and ASI would think much faster than any human could
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (and)

grammarElements.text: but the true separator would be its advantage in intelligence quality
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (but — 대조)

vocabulary.example: The true separator between good and great is consistency.
Having experience gives you an advantage.
The results were quite impressive.

expressions.usage: 겉보기 핵심이 아닌 진짜 핵심을 가리킬 때
이전 논의와의 질적 차이를 강조할 때

patterns.pattern: That sounds [adjective], ... but the true [noun] would be [X], which is something completely different.
patterns.explanation: 일반적 직관을 인정한 뒤("That sounds impressive"), "but"으로 전환하여 진짜 핵심을 제시하는 반전 패턴.
examples: That sounds reasonable, but the true challenge would be implementation, which is something completely different.
That sounds simple, but the true difficulty would be scalability, which is something completely different.

drillQuestions.question: The true _____ would be its advantage in quality.
ASI would think much faster _____ any human could.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 글의 핵심 논점 전환. 속도(speed)에 대한 일반적 직관을 인정한 후, "but"으로 질(quality)이 진짜 핵심임을 드러낸다. em dash(—)로 극적 전환을 표시하는 것이 Tim Urban의 문체 특징.

koreanAlignment.en: That sounds impressive
and ASI would think much faster than any human could
koreanAlignment.ko: ASI는 어떤 인간보다도 훨씬 빠르게 생각할 것입니다

koreanAlignment.en: but the true separator would be
its advantage in intelligence quality
which is something completely different

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 전환(pivot) — 독자의 직관적 이해를 인정한 뒤 진짜 핵심으로 방향 전환

sentences.original: What makes humans so much more intellectually capable than chimps isn't a difference in thinking speed — it's that human brains contain a number of sophisticated cognitive modules that enable things like complex linguistic representations or long-term planning or abstract reasoning, that chimps' brains do not.
sentences.translation: 인간이 침팬지보다 지적으로 훨씬 더 뛰어난 이유는 사고 속도의 차이가 아닙니다 — 인간의 뇌에는 복잡한 언어적 표상, 장기 계획, 추상적 추론 같은 것을 가능하게 하는 여러 정교한 인지 모듈이 포함되어 있고, 침팬지의 뇌에는 그것이 없다는 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: What makes humans so much more intellectually capable than chimps
grammarElements.label: 주어 (what 명사절)

grammarElements.text: isn't a difference in thinking speed
grammarElements.label: 동사 (부정)

grammarElements.text: a number of sophisticated cognitive modules
grammarElements.label: 목적어

vocabulary.example: The software uses sophisticated algorithms.
Cognitive abilities decline with age.
Linguistic diversity is important for cultural heritage.
Abstract reasoning is a key human ability.

expressions.phrase: what makes X so much more Y than Z
expressions.meaning: X를 Z보다 훨씬 더 Y하게 만드는 것
expressions.usage: 차이의 원인을 탐구하는 질문/진술 형태

expressions.example: "a number of"는 "several"과 유사하며, "the number of"(~의 수)와 구분해야 함
expressions.usage: 복수를 나타내는 양적 표현

patterns.pattern: What makes [X] [comparative] isn't [A] — it's that [B].
patterns.explanation: 차이의 원인에 대한 일반적 오해(A)를 부정하고, 진짜 원인(B)을 제시하는 반박 패턴.
examples: What makes great athletes better isn't just talent — it's that they practice with more discipline.
What makes some companies more successful isn't luck — it's that they adapt faster to change.

drillQuestions.question: What _____ humans more capable than chimps?
Human brains contain sophisticated _____ modules.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 글에서 가장 핵심적인 논증. "that chimps' brains do not"는 "that chimps' brains do not contain"에서 contain이 생략된 구조. 세 가지 예시(linguistic representations, long-term planning, abstract reasoning)가 "or"로 병렬 연결된다.

koreanAlignment.en: What makes humans so much more intellectually capable than chimps
koreanAlignment.ko: 인간이 침팬지보다 지적으로 훨씬 더 뛰어난 이유는

koreanAlignment.en: isn't a difference in thinking speed
it's that human brains contain
a number of sophisticated cognitive modules
that enable things like complex linguistic representations
koreanAlignment.ko: 복잡한 언어적 표상 같은 것을 가능하게 하는

koreanAlignment.en: or long-term planning or abstract reasoning
that chimps' brains do not

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 반박(refutation) — 일반적 오해(속도)를 부정하고 진짜 핵심(질)을 제시

sentences.original: Speeding up a chimp's brain by thousands of times wouldn't bring him to our level — even with a decade's time, he wouldn't be able to figure out how to use a set of custom tools to assemble an intricate model, something a human could knock out in a few hours.
sentences.translation: 침팬지의 뇌를 수천 배 빠르게 해도 우리 수준으로 끌어올릴 수는 없을 것입니다 — 10년의 시간이 주어져도, 그는 맞춤 도구 세트를 사용하여 정교한 모형을 조립하는 방법을 알아내지 못할 것이며, 이것은 인간이 몇 시간 만에 해치울 수 있는 일입니다.

grammarElements.text: Speeding up a chimp's brain by thousands of times
grammarElements.label: 주어 (동명사구)

grammarElements.text: wouldn't bring him to our level
grammarElements.label: 동사 (가정법 과거)

grammarElements.text: he wouldn't be able to figure out
grammarElements.label: 주절 (가정법 과거)

grammarElements.text: how to use a set of custom tools to assemble an intricate model
grammarElements.label: 간접의문문 (figure out의 목적어)

grammarElements.text: something a human could knock out in a few hours
grammarElements.label: 동격 (something = model)

vocabulary.example: The clock has an intricate mechanism.
It took hours to assemble the furniture.
She knocked out the report in two hours.
He ordered a custom suit.

expressions.phrase: speed up by thousands of times
expressions.usage: 배수 표현으로 극단적 차이를 강조
능력의 격차를 표현할 때

expressions.phrase: knock out in a few hours
expressions.usage: 비격식적으로 빠른 완성을 표현. knock out은 "기절시키다"에서 비유적으로 확장.

patterns.pattern: [Verb]-ing [X] wouldn't [verb] — even with [time], [subject] wouldn't be able to [verb]
patterns.explanation: 가정법으로 어떤 조치를 취해도 결과가 달라지지 않음을 주장하는 패턴. even으로 양보를 추가하여 불가능성을 강화.
examples: Doubling the budget wouldn't solve the problem — even with unlimited resources, we wouldn't be able to fix it.
Training harder wouldn't make the difference — even with years of practice, the fundamental gap would remain.

drillQuestions.question: Speeding up a chimp's brain _____ bring him to our level.
A human could _____ out the task in a few hours.

sentences.notes: "knock out"은 권투에서 "기절시키다"라는 의미에서 파생되어, 비격식적으로 "빠르게 완성하다"라는 뜻으로 사용된다. "something a human could knock out"에서 관계대명사(that/which)가 생략되었다. 침팬지와 인간의 비교는 나중에 인간과 ASI의 비교로 확장되는 비유의 토대.

koreanAlignment.en: Speeding up a chimp's brain by thousands of times
koreanAlignment.ko: 침팬지의 뇌를 수천 배 빠르게 해도

koreanAlignment.en: wouldn't bring him to our level
koreanAlignment.ko: 우리 수준으로 끌어올릴 수 없을 것입니다

koreanAlignment.en: even with a decade's time
he wouldn't be able to figure out
how to use a set of custom tools
to assemble an intricate model
something a human could knock out in a few hours
koreanAlignment.ko: 인간이 몇 시간 만에 해치울 수 있는 일

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 사고실험(thought experiment) — 침팬지의 뇌 속도를 높이는 가정을 통해 질적 차이를 증명

sentences.original: There are worlds of human cognitive function a chimp will simply never be capable of, no matter how much time he spends trying.
sentences.translation: 침팬지가 아무리 많은 시간을 들여 노력해도 결코 할 수 없는 인간 인지 기능의 세계가 존재합니다.

grammarElements.text: a chimp will simply never be capable of
grammarElements.label: 관계대명사절 (목적격 관계대명사 생략)

grammarElements.text: no matter how much time he spends trying
grammarElements.label: 양보절 (no matter how)

vocabulary.example: There are worlds of difference between the two approaches.
Humans are capable of incredible creativity.
Sleep is essential for cognitive function.

expressions.usage: 양보의 극단적 표현으로, 어떤 노력도 소용없음을 강조
"simply"가 "never"를 강조하여 절대적 불가능성을 표현

patterns.pattern: There are [X] [subject] will simply never be capable of, no matter how much [effort].
patterns.explanation: 절대적 한계를 선언하는 패턴. "no matter how"로 양보의 극한을 표현하여 불가능성을 강화.
examples: There are levels of understanding a beginner will simply never reach, no matter how many books they read.
There are tasks machines will simply never replicate, no matter how advanced they become.

drillQuestions.question: A chimp will simply never be _____ of these functions.
No _____ how much time he spends trying.

sentences.notes: "worlds"는 비유적으로 "매우 넓은 영역"을 의미. "a chimp will simply never be capable of"에서 관계대명사(which/that)가 생략되었고, 전치사 of는 문장 끝에 남아 있다(stranded preposition). 이 문장은 다음 비유(인간이 ASI에 대해서도 같은 한계를 가진다)의 토대를 놓는다.

koreanAlignment.en: There are worlds of human cognitive function
a chimp will simply never be capable of
no matter how much time he spends trying

sentences.original: But it's not just that a chimp can't do what we do, it's that his brain is unable to grasp that those worlds even exist.
sentences.translation: 하지만 침팬지가 우리가 하는 것을 못 하는 것만이 아닙니다. 그의 뇌는 그런 세계가 존재한다는 것조차 파악할 수 없는 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: a chimp can't do what we do
grammarElements.label: that절 (부정되는 내용)

grammarElements.text: it's that his brain is unable to grasp
grammarElements.label: 주절 (진짜 핵심)

vocabulary.example: It's hard to grasp the scale of the universe.
He was unable to solve the problem.

expressions.phrase: it's not just that X, it's that Y
expressions.usage: 핵심을 한 단계 더 깊이 파고드는 논증 패턴. X보다 더 심각한 Y를 제시.
"even"이 불가능성의 정도를 강조

patterns.pattern: It's not just that [X can't do Y], it's that [X] is unable to [verb] that [Y] even [verb].
patterns.explanation: 단순한 능력 부족을 넘어, 그 영역 자체를 인식할 수 없다는 더 근본적인 한계를 제시하는 이중 부정 패턴.
examples: It's not just that students can't solve the equation, it's that they're unable to grasp that the approach exists.
It's not just that companies can't compete, it's that they can't see that the market has shifted.

drillQuestions.question: It's not _____ that a chimp can't do what we do.
His brain is unable to _____ that those worlds exist.

sentences.notes: "it's not just that X, it's that Y" 패턴은 논증을 한 단계 심화하는 강력한 수사법. 첫 번째 that은 "~한 것"이라는 접속사, 두 번째 "that those worlds"의 that도 접속사로 grasp의 목적어절을 이끈다. "even"이 exist를 수식하여 "존재조차"라는 극단적 불가능성을 강조.

koreanAlignment.en: it's not just that
a chimp can't do what we do
koreanAlignment.ko: 침팬지가 우리가 하는 것을 못 하는 것

koreanAlignment.en: it's that his brain is unable to grasp
koreanAlignment.ko: 그의 뇌는 파악할 수 없는 것입니다

koreanAlignment.en: that those worlds even exist

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 점층법(climax) — 단순한 능력 차이에서 인식 자체의 불가능으로 논점을 심화

sentences.original: A chimp can become familiar with what a human is and what a skyscraper is, but he'll never be able to understand that the skyscraper was built by humans.
sentences.translation: 침팬지는 인간이 무엇인지, 고층빌딩이 무엇인지 친숙해질 수 있지만, 그 고층빌딩이 인간에 의해 지어졌다는 것을 결코 이해하지 못할 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: what a human is and what a skyscraper is
grammarElements.label: 간접의문문 (familiar with의 목적어)

grammarElements.text: but he'll never be able to understand
grammarElements.label: 등위절 (but — 대조)

grammarElements.text: that the skyscraper was built by humans
grammarElements.label: that절 (understand의 목적어, 수동태)

vocabulary.example: The name sounds familiar to me.
The city skyline was filled with skyscrapers.

expressions.usage: 점진적으로 익숙해지는 과정을 표현

expressions.phrase: he'll never be able to understand
expressions.meaning: 그는 결코 이해할 수 없을 것이다
expressions.usage: 영구적 불가능성을 미래 시제로 표현

patterns.pattern: [Subject] can [verb] what [X] is, but [subject] will never be able to understand that [clause].
patterns.explanation: 표면적 인식(무엇인지 아는 것)과 깊은 이해(원리를 아는 것)의 차이를 대비하는 패턴.
examples: A child can learn what electricity is, but might never understand that it involves electron flow.
A dog can recognize what a car is, but will never understand that it was manufactured.

drillQuestions.question: A chimp can become _____ with what a human is.
The skyscraper was _____ by humans.

sentences.notes: 이 문장은 표면적 인식과 인과적 이해의 차이를 보여주는 핵심 예시. 침팬지는 인간과 고층빌딩을 "인식"할 수 있지만, "인간이 고층빌딩을 지었다"는 인과 관계는 이해할 수 없다. 이것은 이후 인간이 ASI의 산물을 이해하지 못할 것이라는 비유로 확장된다.

koreanAlignment.en: A chimp can become familiar with
what a human is
and what a skyscraper is
but he'll never be able to understand
that the skyscraper was built by humans
koreanAlignment.ko: 그 고층빌딩이 인간에 의해 지어졌다는 것을

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 구체적 비유(concrete analogy) — 침팬지와 고층빌딩이라는 구체적 예시로 추상적 개념을 이해하기 쉽게 전달

sentences.original: In his world, anything that huge is part of nature, period, and not only is it beyond him to build a skyscraper, it's beyond him to realize that anyone can build a skyscraper.
sentences.translation: 그의 세계에서는, 그렇게 거대한 것은 모두 자연의 일부입니다. 마침표. 그리고 고층빌딩을 짓는 것이 그의 능력 밖일 뿐만 아니라, 누군가가 고층빌딩을 지을 수 있다는 것을 인식하는 것조차 그의 능력 밖인 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: not only is it beyond him to build a skyscraper
grammarElements.label: 도치 구문 (not only + 도치)

grammarElements.text: it's beyond him to realize that anyone can build a skyscraper
grammarElements.label: 주절 (but also 생략)

vocabulary.example: This task is beyond my capabilities.
I'm not going, period.
I realized the importance of education too late.

expressions.meaning: 자연의 일부, 더 이상 논의 없음
expressions.usage: "period"를 문장 끝에 추가하여 단호한 종결을 표현. 미국 영어 특유의 강조법.
주어가 할 수 없는 일을 표현하는 격식/비격식 양용 표현
두 가지 사실을 병렬하여 강조. not only가 문두에 오면 도치가 일어남.

patterns.pattern: Not only is it beyond [someone] to [verb A], it's beyond [someone] to [verb B].
patterns.explanation: "not only A but also B" 구조에서 not only가 문두에 와서 도치(is it)가 일어난 패턴. 두 번째 불가능이 더 근본적임을 보여준다.
examples: Not only is it beyond children to understand quantum physics, it's beyond them to grasp why it matters.
Not only is it beyond the average person to code AI, it's beyond them to understand what AI really does.

drillQuestions.question: Not only _____ it beyond him to build, it's beyond him to realize.
Anything that huge is part of nature, _____.

sentences.notes: "not only is it"에서 "not only"가 문두에 오면서 주어-동사 도치(is it)가 일어난다. "period"는 미국 영어에서 "그게 전부다, 더 이상 논의 없음"이라는 단호한 종결을 나타내는 구어적 표현. "anything that huge"에서 that은 "그렇게"라는 의미의 부사.

koreanAlignment.en: In his world
anything that huge is part of nature, period
koreanAlignment.ko: 그렇게 거대한 것은 모두 자연의 일부입니다, 마침표

koreanAlignment.en: not only is it beyond him to build a skyscraper
koreanAlignment.ko: 고층빌딩을 짓는 것이 능력 밖일 뿐만 아니라

koreanAlignment.en: it's beyond him to realize
that anyone can build a skyscraper
koreanAlignment.ko: 누군가가 고층빌딩을 지을 수 있다는 것을

pronunciationNotes: period: /ˈpɪriəd/ — "피리어드" (강조 용법 시 짧고 강하게 발음)
sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 점층법(climax) + 도치(inversion) — "짓지 못한다"에서 "인식하지 못한다"로 심화, 도치로 강조 극대화

sentences.original: That's the result of a small difference in intelligence quality.
sentences.translation: 이것이 지능의 질에서의 작은 차이가 만들어내는 결과입니다.

grammarElements.text: the result of a small difference
grammarElements.label: 보어

vocabulary.example: Hard work produces good results.
A small difference can have a big impact.

expressions.usage: 원인-결과 관계를 명확하게 요약할 때
차이의 크기를 의도적으로 축소하여 강조 효과를 만들 때

patterns.pattern: That's the result of a [adjective] difference in [noun].
patterns.explanation: 앞선 긴 설명을 한 문장으로 간결하게 요약하는 결론 패턴.
examples: That's the result of a fundamental difference in approach.
That's the result of a slight difference in perspective.

drillQuestions.question: That's the result _____ a small difference.
A small difference in intelligence _____ produces huge effects.

sentences.notes: 의도적으로 짧고 간결한 결론 문장. "small"이 핵심 — 침팬지와 인간의 지능 질적 차이가 "작은" 것이라면, ASI와 인간의 차이는 상상을 초월한다는 것을 암시한다. 뒤에 이어지는 논증의 전제를 깔아 놓는 문장.

koreanAlignment.en: That's the result of
a small difference
in intelligence quality

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 역설적 축소(litotes) — "작은 차이"라고 표현하여 오히려 ASI와의 차이가 얼마나 거대할지 독자가 스스로 추론하게 만듦

sentences.original: And in the scheme of the intelligence range we're talking about today, or even the much smaller range among biological creatures, the chimp-to-human quality intelligence gap is tiny.
sentences.translation: 그리고 오늘 우리가 이야기하는 지능 범위의 맥락에서, 혹은 생물학적 생물 사이의 훨씬 더 작은 범위에서조차, 침팬지에서 인간까지의 질적 지능 격차는 아주 작습니다.

grammarElements.text: And in the scheme of the intelligence range
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (맥락)

grammarElements.text: or even the much smaller range among biological creatures
grammarElements.label: 삽입 부사구 (or even)

vocabulary.example: In the grand scheme of things, this is a minor issue.
The product comes in a wide range of colors.
There is a growing gap between rich and poor.
The difference is tiny compared to the whole.

expressions.usage: 더 넓은 맥락에서 비교하여 상대적 크기를 표현
더 좁은/넓은 범위를 추가로 제시할 때

patterns.pattern: In the scheme of [X], the [A]-to-[B] gap is [adjective].
patterns.explanation: 더 큰 맥락에서 보면 특정 차이가 상대적으로 작다/크다는 것을 주장하는 관점 전환 패턴.
examples: In the scheme of cosmic time, human history is tiny.
In the scheme of global economics, this budget gap is insignificant.

drillQuestions.question: In the _____ of the intelligence range, the gap is tiny.
The chimp-to-human intelligence gap is _____.

sentences.notes: "in the scheme of"는 "in the grand scheme of things"의 변형으로, 더 넓은 맥락에서의 상대적 크기를 나타낸다. "chimp-to-human"은 하이픈으로 연결된 복합 형용사. 이 문장의 핵심 메시지: 침팬지-인간 격차조차 전체 지능 범위에서는 미미하다는 것.

koreanAlignment.en: in the scheme of the intelligence range
we're talking about today
or even the much smaller range among biological creatures
koreanAlignment.ko: 혹은 생물학적 생물 사이의 훨씬 더 작은 범위에서조차

koreanAlignment.en: the chimp-to-human quality intelligence gap
is tiny

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 스케일 전환(scale shift) — 독자의 관점을 확대하여, 익숙한 차이가 사실은 미미함을 보여줌

sentences.original: To absorb how big a deal a superintelligent machine would be, imagine one on the dark green step two steps above humans on that staircase.
sentences.translation: 초지능 기계가 얼마나 대단한 것인지 이해하려면, 그 계단에서 인간보다 두 단계 위에 있는 짙은 초록색 단계에 있는 기계를 상상해 보세요.

grammarElements.text: To absorb how big a deal a superintelligent machine would be
grammarElements.label: 목적 부사구 (to부정사 — 목적)

grammarElements.text: two steps above humans on that staircase
grammarElements.label: 전치사구 (위치 상세)

vocabulary.example: It takes time to absorb complex information.
Getting this contract is a big deal for us.
She walked up the spiral staircase.

expressions.usage: "how + 형용사 + a/an + 명사" 구조로 감탄/의문의 정도를 표현
시각적 사고실험을 유도할 때

patterns.pattern: To absorb how [adjective] [noun] would be, imagine [object] ...
patterns.explanation: 추상적 개념을 시각적 비유로 전환하도록 독자에게 요청하는 명령형 패턴.
examples: To absorb how fast light travels, imagine a beam circling the Earth seven times per second.
To absorb how vast the ocean is, imagine all the continents submerged.

drillQuestions.question: To absorb _____ big a deal it would be, imagine ...
Imagine one on the step two steps _____ humans.

sentences.notes: "how big a deal"은 "how + 형용사 + a + 명사" 구조로, 감탄문에서 자주 사용. "absorb"는 여기서 물리적 "흡수"가 아닌 "정보를 받아들이다/이해하다"라는 비유적 의미. 이 문장은 이전 포스트에서 소개한 "지능 계단" 시각 자료를 참조.

koreanAlignment.en: To absorb
how big a deal a superintelligent machine would be
imagine one
on the dark green step
two steps above humans on that staircase
koreanAlignment.ko: 그 계단에서 인간보다 두 단계 위에

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 시각화(visualization) — 독자에게 직접 상상하도록 요청하여 추상적 개념을 구체화

sentences.original: This machine would be only slightly superintelligent, but its increased cognitive ability over us would be as vast as the chimp-human gap we just described.
sentences.translation: 이 기계는 단지 약간만 초지능적일 뿐이지만, 우리에 대한 그것의 향상된 인지 능력은 방금 설명한 침팬지-인간 격차만큼 광대할 것입니다.

grammarElements.text: but its increased cognitive ability over us
grammarElements.label: 주어 (대조)

grammarElements.text: the chimp-human gap we just described
grammarElements.label: 보어 (관계대명사 목적격 생략)

vocabulary.example: The temperature dropped slightly.
The Sahara is a vast desert.
The increased budget allowed for more research.

expressions.usage: "only"가 "slightly"를 한정하여 의도적으로 최소한으로 표현
원급 비교로 두 격차의 동등함을 표현
"over"가 상대적 우위를 나타냄

patterns.pattern: [X] would be only slightly [adjective], but its [noun] would be as [adjective] as [Y].
patterns.explanation: "단지 약간"이라는 축소와 "~만큼 크다"라는 확대를 대조하여 놀라움을 유발하는 패턴.
examples: The upgrade would be only slightly more expensive, but its impact would be as significant as a complete redesign.
The change would be only slightly noticeable, but its long-term effects would be as profound as a revolution.

drillQuestions.question: This machine would be only _____ superintelligent.
Its cognitive ability would be as _____ as the chimp-human gap.

sentences.notes: 이 문장의 충격적 핵심: "약간만" 초지능적인 기계조차 인간과 침팬지만큼의 격차를 가진다. "only slightly"는 의도적 축소(understatement)로, "as vast as"와 대조되어 독자에게 충격을 준다. "we just described"에서 관계대명사가 생략되었다.

koreanAlignment.en: This machine would be only slightly superintelligent
koreanAlignment.ko: 이 기계는 단지 약간만 초지능적일 뿐이지만

koreanAlignment.en: but its increased cognitive ability over us
would be as vast as
the chimp-human gap we just described

sentences.rhetoricalDevice: 축소법(understatement) + 대비(contrast) — "약간만"이라는 축소가 "광대한 격차"와 대조되어 압도적 충격을 만듦
